'''Awdrysta Pina''', known as the '''Green Blade''', was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who led the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] as its [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] during the time of the [[First Great Schism]].

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], Awdrysta Pina was a member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], achieving the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]<ref name="Warfare" /> and ultimately [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]]<ref name="FaD">[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook]]</ref> during the years before the [[First Great Schism]]. Despite having a taste for battle, the grim Jedi spent his time away from [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], the Jedi world, and preferred to wander the long forgotten space lanes of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].<ref name="Warfare" /> During his time as a Jedi, Pina constructed<ref name="FaD" /> twin [[Force-imbued sword/Legends|katanas]],<ref name="dfs4">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050526a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 4: Of the Crudest Matter}}</ref> imbuing them with the Force and channeling his power through twin emerald focusing crystals.<ref name="FaD" /> Despite his craving for battle, Pina believed that order and structure were the underlying base of civilization, and therefore responded with earnest when the Order' leaders summoned him to Ossus to combat the [[Legions of Lettow]].<ref name="Warfare" />

Leading the Jedi forces against the Legions and their leader, [[Xendor]], Pina crushed resistance on [[Battle of Chandrila (First Great Schism)|Chandrila]], [[Battle of Brentaal IV (First Great Schism)|Brentaal IV]], [[Battle of Metellos (First Great Schism)|Metellos]] and [[Battle of Coruscant (First Great Schism)|Coruscant]] before tracking Xendor to [[Columus/Legends|Columus]]. During the [[Battle of Columus|fighting]], Pina's forces slew fierce warriors such as [[Sethul Asaiage]] and [[Tun Bohoi]] before Pina finally struck down Xendor himself.<ref name="Warfare" />

Despite the victory and the death of the Legions' leader, Pina had not sated his need to destroy all those involved. Tracking Xendor's lover [[Arden Lyn]] to [[Irkalla]]<ref name="Warfare" /> in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]],<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Pina [[Duel on Irkalla|attacked]] Lyn but his katana shattered upon the powerful [[Kashi Mer Dynasty|Kashi Mer]] [[Kashi Mer talisman|talisman]] she wore around her neck. Mortally wounded by the flying shards of his own blade, Pina's desire to end Lyn's life did not die. Drawing upon the Force, Pina employed the rarely-used technique called [[Morichro/Legends|morichro]] to cease all of Lyn's body functions, ultimately resulting in the stoppage of her heart. Pina died during the process, and Lyn would have too if not for the intervention of unknown individuals. Lyn's body was suspended in a stasis field deep within a tomb, only to reawaken some [[4 BBY/Legends|24,500 years]] later during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Warfare" />

==Behind the scenes==
According to ''[[The Emperor's Pawns]]'' author [[Abel G. Peña]], Pina's name originally was "Waldan L'lacielo," an anagram of fellow author [[Daniel Wallace]]. This name was changed in editing; Peña later went on to split the anagram and use it for the first names of [[Grievous/Legends|General Grievous's]] victims [[Waldan Bridger]] and [[L'lacielo Sageon]].<ref name=" Endnotes for The Story of General Grievous, Part 2">{{Blog|abelgpena|55/comments|Endnotes for The Story of General Grievous, Part 2: The Knight Slayer}}</ref>
